All right, let's rock and roll. So, lead gen videos. There are different types of videos I'm gonna go over so that you understand the strategy and the taxes behind making videos that are going to convert for you. All right, and so there's two primary objectives you have with a video. You're gonna have pure opt-in videos, which the two types that have worked best, historically are group workout videos and pitch videos. I'm gonna show you examples of each of those after looking at literally millions of dollars of spending, which one worked the best, and I'll show you just how you can deconstruct it. What's really cool about both of these is that there's a formula that is really repeatable that you can use over and over again for each of them, which is exciting for me after looking at all the data. All right, the next is, if you want like higher quality applications. So these are always typically gonna be more costly, and there's two styles here that I recommend. The first is gonna be the testimony of video, which is a five step epiphany bridge, and I'll break down each one of the steps that you're gonna have to cover in the video, and two styles of doing them, which is more professional style or more of an organic self-made video. Both of them tend to convert well. It just gives you a little bit different look and feel. The next is long form adjugate videos. I wanna be clear, these are the hardest ones to make because they just take the most time, they take the most scripting, it has to be really useful. There's a lot of pieces to it, but I just wanna cover all of the different types of videos and the objectives they serve. All right, so for opt-ins, because this is where the majority of people literally make their money, I wanna cover this first. And so for this, I wanna cover the six questions, what, why, when, where, who, and how, all right? So what, so this is what I wanna show you right now are these are the top video ads that we have over the last two years seen. And after clicking to each one of these, you will see that it is group workouts that are being done in unison over and over and over again, the same thing has been repeated. People moving in synchrony, in unison, the same thing at the same time draws people's attention. And it just freaking works, right? You can see it right here. These are all the ads that have converted best that fall into the first category. And I just wanna be clear that 75, 80% of the best ads total have been these style ads. And the nice thing is that these are some of the easiest ones to make and capture. All right, here's some more. So I like showing these because there's different look and feels that I want you to see here so that you can almost immediately identify what's going to be a winner, right? Now, and here's even more, right? I wanted to give you this gigantic display of all of them. But you can see here that all of these are the same where people are doing it. So here's the formula for it, all right? It's synchrony, social proof. The reason it's social proof is because you're showing that you have a lot of people. So the more people doing it, the better. It's also from a human mentality. We want to do what everyone else is doing and we want to fit in. We want to be in the in group. And so subconsciously people are drawn to those types of things and want to be included in it. All right, you also see that there's energy. There's movement. This is really important. The things that do best are the ones where people are moving together, not just like sitting in a circle together or doing wall sets together, but actually squatting, doing pushups, doing bicycles with their bicycle setups or whatever. Things that are movement and the more in synchrony they can be, the more effective they will be as an advertisement, right? Also shows community because when people do things together, it means they are in, well, synchrony, but there's a relationship. And so it truly shows a group better than almost anything on a subconscious animal level. And that is why this stuff works. And as much as you're like, why is Alex talking about animal and evolution and all that kind of stuff, it's because you're amygdala, which is the tiny little reptile brain in your brain, is the one that makes all of your decisions for you and then you rationalize them later. And so these are the things that that little brain is looking for to say, hey, that looks good. We should do that, all right? Now after the ABCD of why these work, the last thing is that you always want to have clear call to actions. And that goes for any advertisement, but it's just worth mentioning again. All right, so what are the who? Having a cheap old iPhone or iPad on the class floor and requiring your trainers to record the finishers or any group exercises and trying to get people to do it on the same cadence by doing countdowns and whatnot works very, very well. People also like it a lot too. It keeps everyone together, so you kind of build community while you're doing it and you can record advertisements. All right, now, if you have them do this at every one of their sessions, you should be getting six to eight new videos per day. This is called a process. If we do this on a continual basis, you don't need to spend all this time trying to collect and make ads for yourself if this is just part of the process of your facility. If you get six to eight new ads a day, you're gonna have 40 by the end of the week and you will be able to find five or six really good ones where there was a lot more people, there was good energy, and people are really doing things in unison. All right, one thing that I would do here is I would try and take these from different angles. So every day I would have the different trainers take it from a specific angle. So that can be from in the middle, doing a loop. It can be from the corner, so you can see the whole class. It can be from an elevated surface. You wanna take from one side of the gym, from another side of the gym. You wanna walk into the gym while people are doing it. All of those are different variations of the same concept, but will make the ads look somewhat different for you. All right, now, to motivate them, giving a small bonus to the trainer who makes the top video that performs, give them like 50 bucks, and that will involve them in the whole advertising process so that they are just motivated and incentivized to do a good job with it and really go the extra mile. What I have also noticed is that most trainers love showing what they're doing to their clients, to or with their clients, and so most of them are already kinda doing this half the time anyways, so we're just putting a deliberate process in place. They can also use Instagram as well to record story ads so they can be native, and then you can have them saved to the phone so that you can use those for story ads as well on IG. All right, doing it during or after class, especially on Saturday morning classes, since those tend to be the biggest for most people, that's where you really wanna make sure you get them, cause those just in general tend to be the biggest. Make sure that your clients sign a release form, if you're gonna be using this for advertisements, they should already be inside of the contract when they sign up with you, but just as an added reminder. All right, when? So best times to do it are synchronized warmups, so if people are doing warmups together, which happens a lot, that is a huge opportunity every time you do it, all right, so that's number one. Second is any kinda met consequence, or if you do finishers or group things like everyone's doing dummy jacks in unison or everyone's doing, I mean, there's literally a million things they could do, just have them do it in unison, and those are the two, are three primary ways that they can do it in finishers, right? A really cool thing is when you do partnered exercises, so if you look back at the old video examples that I just used that were the best ones, about a third of them were partnered, so people throwing a ball back and forth to each other, or people doing sit-ups and giving high fives to the people who are standing on their feet, things like that also show even more community, and they tend to work well if you're trying to keep a cadence. All right, where? There's different places you can do this, one is inside the gym, the second is outside the gym, so if you wanna have a different look or feel to your ads and you feel like you've done a lot of ads inside of your gym from all the different angles, the next one to do would be outside of the parking lot, so I mean, I know at least for me, I had a decent sized parking lot that I would have people do different things in outside, and so that gave us just a different look and feel, and I could even include the signage above the door inside my advertisement so it made it look even more legitimate. Like I said, here's just a couple examples of different angles that you can consider inside or outside of your gym to give it even more variety. All right, how? So you wanna make sure that you're doing your iPhone sideways and vertical, both of those types of ads have converted, just looking at the best winners, most of them were sideways, so if you're gonna do a split, do 70, 30, and that's probably gonna be your best bet. I think the reason that it's second highest is that there's just not as many people who are as good at being energetic on video. That's just really what it comes down to. Now, why these work is because seeing the owner of the proprietor builds trust, seeing a facility builds trust, seeing an in-shaped person increases authority, and trust that you can actually deliver on the thing that you're saying, right? If you can have clients in the background, there's looking for a specific video that I know did really well, I think Joe Green had it, where he was inside of his gym and he did it in a selfie mode, and he had probably 15 or 20 clients behind him, and he's like, hey, everybody, how great is the Six Week Challenge? Everyone's like, yay! And that works insanely well, because you're kinda getting the group and the social proof while you're also edifying yourself. Now, who, and this is really important. It can be you, and if it's not you, it needs to be this, which is a good looking trainer. Women tend to do better than men. And what I wanna say here is, I'm purely talking advertising. I do not favor men over women, women over men. It is irrelevant to me, I'm just looking at data. There is a reason that advertising agencies hire models. There's a reason that big brands hire models for their commercials, because good looking people tend to convert better than not good looking people. And especially when what you're selling is being good looking, which is in some ways what fitness is, right? So some other things you might wanna notice is that bright color clothing tends to do best. It's just bright, it's movement, it's color. It also tends to evoke happier thoughts, so they'll have positive associations with you. Smile, I know that sounds crazy, but you actually have to look likable. Insane energy. So think like a crack addict. You need to, like, you will feel not cool doing this. So I just wanna be clear. If you wanna have pitch styles at work, pitch style videos at work, you need to feel like you're a crack addict. Because on video, you automatically seem like one-tenth as energetic as you do normally. So to seem energetic on video, you need to be like larger than life in person, okay? Now, you'll notice which each of these, if you, when you watch the video, so you can get an idea of how they work so well, is you wanna have a silly entrance, but it shouldn't be longer than two to three seconds tops. Don't waste too much time on your pattern interrupt. It's only there to grab someone's attention. So two seconds is probably the best, three at the longest. Okay, so that's where waving hands, using flashcards, doing a cartwheel before someone comes up, doing the worm, if one of your trainers has that skill, twerking, doing the moonwalk, dancing of any kind. You notice that was kind of a theme of these things, is that, you know, James did a flip, and then the other guy was doing the dancing. If you see some of the other videos, some of the girls are doing the Charlie, I think it's called the Charlie Brown. You know what, I'm not even hip, like the kids these days, but you get my point, is that just having a little jig, a little something, just makes you fun, but it also breaks someone's attention, so they're going to be more likely to watch it, right? Big thing here is that you don't wanna waste time, it says move your ass, it means don't waste time on this thing, like it should be no longer than three seconds, absolutely, you should shoot for two, because people don't have that much total attention, so you've got four seconds total to actually convince someone that they should keep watching. So four seconds is just from what I know from phone sales, you have four seconds before someone is gonna choose, is gonna choose whether they're actually gonna take the phone call. All right, so you've got two seconds to pretty much break their attention, and another two seconds to look interesting enough for them to keep watching. Now, have them cheer, and then they can go on with their class. I did an entire series of ads one night for two hours, and I held all the classes, and they had to be silent with no music. It was like the weirdest thing, it was horrible, don't make that mistake. The third place is obviously outside the gym, and the fourth is kind of a transition, so if you're pitching, you can you yourself walk someone into the facility with you, and when people, how to do this sideways iPhone video, selfie style seems to be the highest converting. There are some that are vertical, it's just that the vertical memes tend to be a little bit more difficult to make, and look easy to read, and so I would recommend doing them as a sideways selfie style in your hand. The other option here is to have someone holding it for you so that you are in the frame. What I wanna be clear here is how big should you be in the frame? You should shoot for peck line and up, because you're gonna be really small on someone's phone, which is where the majority of your traffic is gonna be coming from, and so you want to be, you wanna fill up most of the frame, so they actually see your facial expression, see your energy like that, so if you're gonna run into the frame, you can run from far away, but where you should finish should be peck line and up, really like shoulders, so they can see your hands, see your animated, but that's gonna be the majority of the video. All right, props. This is just something that I didn't notice this until I looked at all of them. Buying one of those visual fat blobs seems to convert very well, so I'd recommend buying one. Make sure you transcribe the video underneath. Facebook usually has automatic transcriptions available. You can do that. This is what I've always done for years, because I am probably catering to myself. I hate listening to actual videos. That being said, after reviewing a lot of other fitness ads for large fitness companies, a lot of them that are influencer based actually didn't transcribe, so this one I wanna add as a caveat. I transcribe all of our videos because I think that people don't want to print selfie style videos. Typically you'll have less effects on these videos because you're really talking to them rather than displaying people who are not saying anything, and so you actually kind of need the effects and the text to do some of the selling and talking for you when you're in a group setting or showing group workouts or group synchrony, whereas in a pitch stop video you're doing the selling so it doesn't require as many effects. Right? So that concludes the two primary styles of highest converting videos, and that is where I would focus the majority of my attention is creating variety in those two styles and creating a process consistently at your facility so that you can have the ultimate amount of collateral to be able to test. And if you feel like when you ask for six to eight, they do 68 shitty ones, then just ask them to do one really good one per shift if that's what it comes down to. But at the very least if you just get two per trainer, per day, you're looking at four a day, one from the morning, one from the afternoon, probably from your busiest class, and it's still gonna give you a ton of creative content that you don't need to go spend time to go collect. All right? Awesome sauce.
